name: The Prediction Engine
description: Brain minimizes prediction error. All emotion is the gap between expected and actual.

primitives.description: Internal model of how everything works. Contains beliefs and expectations. Resists rapid change.
primitives.behaviorBias: generates predictions about what happens next

primitives.description: Compares prediction to reality. Error size IS the emotion. Large error = strong feeling.
primitives.behaviorBias: signals that something unexpected happened — demands attention

primitives.description: Updates beliefs when error is accepted. This IS learning. Conservative — takes repeated errors to change deep beliefs.
primitives.behaviorBias: absorbs new information, adjusts expectations, reconsiders beliefs

primitives.description: Instead of updating beliefs, ACT on the world to match prediction. Flee to restore predicted safety. Conform to restore predicted acceptance.
primitives.behaviorBias: acts to make reality match prediction — flee, conform, seek, avoid

primitives.description: Controls trust in predictions vs new data. High precision on predictions = ignore contradictory evidence (confirmation bias).
primitives.behaviorBias: trusts existing predictions, dismisses contradictory evidence
